Case Study UX · Automotive · EV 2022–23
🏆 Make a Difference Project Award · Yuj Designs

Automobile Driven Fleet

An EV subscription platform for rideshare drivers — designed from discovery to delivery in partnership with Uber for the Washington, DC market.

Role
Solo UX Designer
+ 1 UI Designer
Company
Yuj Designs
2022–2023
Platform
Web App · Mobile
Cadence
6 sprints
2 weeks each
1st
Of its kind EV subscription platform for rideshare drivers
6
Agile sprints — each delivering a fully tested module
3
Steps to sign up — leveraging existing Uber credentials
🏆
Make a Difference Project Award — Yuj Designs, 2022
01 — Overview

What is this project?

An automobile company partnered with Uber to offer exclusive electric vehicle subscriptions to rideshare earners in the Washington, DC area — a first-of-its-kind EV fleet experience.

The Opportunity
First-of-its-kind EV fleet for Uber drivers
Join forces with Uber to let drivers rent and experience new EV vehicles through a subscription model in Washington, DC.
The Approach
Futuristic and iterative
A new module was ideated, designed, and tested every 2 weeks via agile sprints — adapting the service around real driver needs.
02 — The Problem

Uber drivers had no streamlined path to EV subscription

The service was new, complex, and completely unknown to drivers. There was no prior interface, no established trust, and no guidance on whether the EV would even work in their area — creating massive friction before a single driver signed up.

Problem 01
Discovery friction
Drivers didn't know if the EV subscription was available in their area or relevant to their income.
Problem 02
Complex sign-up
The sign-up process needed to leverage existing Uber data — reducing friction to just 3 simple steps.
Problem 03
No post-signup visibility
After signing up, drivers had no visibility into vehicle availability, subscription status, or maintenance.
03 — My Role & Process

How I worked

Solo UX Designer working in 2-week agile sprints with evening client brainstorm sessions every cycle. Each sprint delivered a fully ideated, wireframed, prototyped and user-tested module.

1
Discovery
Research & planning
2
Content Mapping
User stories & journey
3
Task Flows
Wireframes & variations
4
Prototyping
High-fidelity design
5
Usability Testing
Online testing & iteration
6
Handoff
Refinement & delivery
04 — Research

Understanding the driver

Research was conducted across multiple methods to understand driver behaviour, expectations, and pain points when switching to an EV subscription model.

Quantitative
Surveys
Driver preferences, EV familiarity, and concerns about subscription commitments were gathered via structured surveys.
Desk Research
Secondary Research
EV adoption trends, competitor services, and Uber driver behaviour studies informed foundational design decisions.
Validation
Usability Testing
Online usability tests were run per sprint to validate each new module — ensuring real drivers could complete key tasks.
05 — Sprint Planning

6 sprints · 2 weeks each

Each sprint covered ideation, wireframing, prototyping and online user-testing for one feature module — ensuring nothing went to hi-fi without validation.

06 — Information Architecture

Content mapping across the full driver journey

Mapped user stories for each stage of the entire driver journey — from first discovery to active subscription. Key user story: "As a driver, I'd like to understand if this service is available and right for me before committing."

07 — Ideation

Task flow variations

Multiple task flow variations were explored for each feature module before committing to a direction — ensuring the chosen path was validated against driver mental models.

08 — Final Design

Two core features delivered

Feature 01 — Vehicle Marketplace

Selected Uber drivers receive an exclusive invitation to browse the EV fleet available in their service area. The marketplace surfaces vehicle specs, pricing, and subscription benefits upfront — so drivers can make an informed decision before committing.

Feature 02 — Waitlist Sign-up

Drivers authenticate using their existing Uber credentials — no duplicate data entry. The sign-up flow is streamlined to just 3 steps: verify identity, add payment details, and join the waitlist.

09 — Recognition

Make a Difference Project Award

⭐
Recognition
Make a Difference
Project Award
This project was awarded the Make a Difference Project Award at Yuj Designs — recognising its impact in designing a first-of-its-kind EV subscription experience for rideshare drivers.
Takeaway 01
Sprint-based UX design enables rapid iteration without sacrificing depth.
Takeaway 02
Leveraging existing driver data (Uber) dramatically reduced onboarding friction.
Takeaway 03
Designing for a novel product means building trust before building features.
